summary: OpenWeatherMap provides weather data and forecasts through a REST API.

steps.title: Create an OpenWeatherMap Account
steps.description: Go to the OpenWeatherMap website and sign up for a free account.
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the OpenWeatherMap homepage with a blue header and sign up form.
steps.expected_result: You have created an account and are logged in.

steps.title: Navigate to API Keys Section
steps.description: After logging in, go to the API keys section to generate your key.
steps.screenshot_hint: Look for the 'API keys' link in your account dashboard navigation menu.
steps.expected_result: You are on the API keys management page.

steps.title: Generate Your API Key
steps.description: Click the 'Generate' button to create a new API key for your application.
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see a form with a 'Generate' button and fields for key name and permissions.
steps.expected_result: Your new API key is displayed and ready to use.

common_errors.fix: Your API key is invalid or missing. Verify that you're using the correct API key from your OpenWeatherMap account.
Your API key doesn't have permission to access the requested resource. Check that you're using a valid key with appropriate permissions.
You've exceeded the rate limit for your subscription plan. Consider upgrading your plan or implementing request throttling.

pricing_info: OpenWeatherMap free tier: 1,000 API calls/day. Paid plans start at $40/month for 10,000 calls.
